sourceOfTruth.behavior: Flutter implementations in lib/core/widgets/catch_*.dart own runtime behavior, accessibility, layout, and state.
sourceOfTruth.tokens: design/tokens/catch.tokens.json owns primitive token values; generated Dart/CSS and Figma variables mirror it.
sourceOfTruth.designTools: Figma and Claude Design may compose these contracts, but they do not own production widget implementation.
sourceOfTruth.handoff: Design handoffs should name contract ids and props. Arbitrary JSX/CSS is proposal material until mapped to these contracts.

rules: The owning product surface keeps domain value, validation, and async state.
The field owns row geometry, disclosure chrome, adornment placement, and status presentation.
New modes or semantic slots require the Flutter API, component contract, Widgetbook coverage, tests, and generated facade inventory to change together.
The section owns grouping, dividers, contained chrome, header composition, and field-row focus or error treatment.
Product surfaces keep route layout and domain state outside the section contract.
Use field sections for data entry and management surfaces, never as a generic storytelling container.

components.name: CatchBadge
components.summary: Small status badge for compact metadata, state, and count-adjacent labels.

members.summary: Anchored 99+ count marker that overlays a child icon or navigation glyph and hides when count is zero.
Dot-only status marker used inside rows, badges, and presence-style metadata.
Quiet unboxed status made from a semantic status dot and wrapping supporting copy.

notes: Use for short factual labels only; long prose belongs in supporting text.
Use activity accent overrides only when the badge meaning is activity-scoped.
Use the named solid, live, and on-dark constructors; those fixed recipes are not generic tone values.
CatchCountBadge owns numeric overlays, while CatchInlineStatus owns quiet unboxed status.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchSectionLabel
components.summary: Structural eyebrow or compact metadata label that introduces a section without communicating status.

notes: Use to introduce hierarchy or metadata context; use catch.badge for state, status, counts, or alerts.
Keep copy short and scannable. Long explanatory text belongs in supporting copy.
Surface adapters own casing, color, spacing, and element choice while preserving non-interactive semantics.

components.name: CatchButton
components.summary: Canonical action button for product CTAs, destructive actions, and secondary commands.

slots.name: icon
slots.notes: Prefer Icon(CatchIcons.*) sized by the primitive.

members.summary: Text-only action member for inline actions, dialog actions, top-bar text actions, and compact retry links, with optional FocusNode forwarding for canonical composite focus chrome.
Direct button label renderer for icon, loading, and typography composition.
Direct loading-dot renderer used inside loading buttons.

notes: Every product action still needs action cardinality and controller ownership outside this visual contract.
Use accentColor only for activity-scoped primary actions.
Use CatchTextButton for text-only actions; do not create separate inline-action wrappers.

components.name: CatchChip
components.summary: Canonical compact-label primitive for passive tags, parent-owned choices, registry-backed activities, and removable values behind explicit named constructors.

props.name: label
props.notes: Required by tag, selectable, and removable; optional override for activity.

props.name: activityKind
props.notes: Required only by CatchChip.activity.

props.name: onChanged
props.notes: Required only by CatchChip.selectable; state remains parent-owned.

props.name: onTap
props.notes: Optional interaction only for CatchChip.activity.

props.name: onRemove
props.notes: Required only by CatchChip.removable; the whole chip is the removal action.

members.summary: Form-field wrapper for single- and multi-select chip groups.

notes: Choose tag, selectable, activity, or removable by semantic intent; do not add boolean/callback combinations to an unrestricted constructor.
Selected choices use a solid fill, inverse ink, subtle selected shadow, and press-only scale. Selection state is always parent-owned.
Use badges for compact status metadata, compact controls for floating actions, identity switchers for current-context menus, and progress cues for sequence state.
Use OptionCard when each choice needs explanatory copy.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchEmptyState
components.summary: Canonical success-empty-state composition for stacked or inline empty content, optional icon, optional action, and optional contained surface.

members.summary: Direct empty-state content renderer for title, message, action, and layout variants.
Direct empty-state icon renderer for plain and bubble icon treatments.

notes: Use only for genuinely empty successful data states, not load failures.
Use CatchErrorState or CatchInlineErrorState for error branches with retry.

components.name: CatchErrorState
components.summary: Canonical branded app-facing error surface family for full-screen, inline, compact, scaffold, sliver, and icon-only placements.

slots.name: secondaryAction
slots.notes: Use only for a secondary recovery command; primary retry stays in onRetry.

members.summary: Shared branded icon medallion composed by app-facing and framework-facing error surfaces.
Route/root-tab placement adapter for full-screen app data-load failures.
Sliver placement adapter for branded load failures inside CustomScrollView routes.
Inline section/card placement adapter for retryable failures when the rest of the screen remains usable.
Framework-build-error fallback that stays intentionally boring and robust while composing the shared branded error medallion.
Tokenized debug-only framework error disclosure used by CatchFrameworkErrorView.
Direct branded error-body renderer used by error placement adapters.

notes: Use this family for app-facing data-load failures with mapped copy and retry callbacks.
Use scaffold and sliver members for placement only; do not create separate error visual systems.
Keep framework crash surfaces separate in CatchFrameworkErrorView, but compose the shared error icon medallion.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchErrorBanner
components.summary: Persistent inline error-feedback banner family for mutation and async failures inside existing page content.

members.summary: Persistent inline mutation-error adapter that renders CatchErrorBanner when a MutationState has an error.
Transient snackbar boundary for one mutation's pending-to-error transition.
Transient snackbar boundary for several mutations that should share one error-feedback policy.

notes: Use for persistent inline failures inside already-rendered content.
Use CatchErrorState for data-load branches that replace content.
Use the mutation listener members for transient snackbar policy, not for visual layout.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchNotice
components.summary: Ambient app notice family for queued/persistent top-of-screen status, success, warning, danger, and event notices.

members.summary: Overlay boundary that renders persistent notices plus the queued current notice above app content.

notes: Use for ambient app-level notices that float above existing content.
Use CatchErrorBanner for inline persistent form/section failures and CatchErrorState for blocking data-load failures.

components.name: CatchField
components.summary: Unified handoff field primitive with one canonical single-line empty Add state across direct inputs and addable disclosures, stable native-input identity, root-owned inactive/error/active label color resolution, one caption-reserve 18.9 px value-line trailing lane, exactly one visible saving indicator, shared section-edge overlay geometry, a dedicated natural-height content row, one-gesture press-to-focus transfer, native cursor placement, height-neutral clear targets, full-row animated disclosure drawers, wrapping and per-item-accent choices, helper and badge metadata, bounded steppers, exact toggles, async status, direct and explicit-save text editing, select triggers, and add actions.

props.name: titleMaxLines
props.notes: Legacy value-row constructors default to 1. CatchField.content defaults to 2.

props.name: bodyMaxLines
props.notes: Legacy value-row constructors default to 2. CatchField.content defaults to 3.

props.name: emphasis
props.notes: Content rows keep their fixed title/supporting typography; emphasis controls top-aligned versus combined-block-centered leading/trailing slots.

props.name: leading
props.notes: Caller-owned semantic leading content for read, content, navigation, and action rows. Mutually exclusive with icon.

props.name: addable
props.notes: Disclosure-field opt-in to the shared single-line empty Add presentation. Direct editable row inputs derive the same presentation automatically while empty and inactive.

props.name: isOptional
props.notes: Presentation and accessibility copy only; renders the inline ' · Optional' suffix and never controls whether a selection may be cleared.

props.name: allowEmptySelection
props.notes: CatchField.choices selection policy for both single and multi controls. Independent from isOptional.

props.name: status
props.notes: Implicit or closed-field save feedback in the trailing lane. When an explicit commit bar is visible and saving, that bar becomes the sole progress owner; saved remains a trailing success tick.

props.name: isLoading
props.notes: Locks disclosure interactions during an async explicit save. A visible commit bar renders the sole 13 px spinner inside Done; when the bar is not visible the trailing lane preserves one progress indicator.

props.name: emptyValueText
props.notes: Optional override for root-localized 'Add {field}' copy. It appears only while an editable row is empty and inactive; it does not replace the native input or its focused hint.

props.name: inputHint
props.notes: Input-only example or instruction shown after focus. At rest, empty editable rows use the shared Add presentation instead.

props.name: badgeLabel
props.notes: CatchField.toggle title-row metadata rendered through CatchBadge.

props.name: itemAccent
props.notes: CatchField.choices forwards a resolved item accent to CatchChip.selectable.

props.name: itemTitle
props.notes: CatchField.optionCards title resolver for mutually exclusive explanatory choices.

props.name: itemDescription
props.notes: CatchField.optionCards supporting-copy resolver. Every description stays inside its option's clickable CatchOptionCard.

slots.name: leading
slots.notes: Use for semantic data blocks that cannot be expressed by a single icon.

slots.name: control
slots.notes: Use for option groups, chips, steppers, or other controlled primitives.

slots.name: supporting
slots.notes: In explicit-save editors this follows the value lane and precedes secondary and commit actions.

slots.name: secondaryAction
slots.notes: In explicit-save editors this follows supporting metadata and feedback and precedes Cancel/Done.

members.summary: Ambient geometry owner that independently publishes horizontal content flushing and active-overlay bleed. CatchSectionFocusSurface field-row surfaces publish one hairline of bleed so child and section borders share one edge even in direct compositions; omitted bleed preserves the divided flush-row default.
Field-label atom for required, optional, error, and large label states.
Public field-row anatomy member used by CatchField for leading, content, trailing, add, and tappable row states.

Public trailing-slot member used by CatchField for value text, chevrons, toggles, clear actions, validation, and custom trailing content. CatchField composes non-centered trailing content into one lane after a single 18 px caption reserve and centers it within the 18.9 px value line. Saving progress appears here only when no explicit commit bar is visible.
Animated field-family saving and saved feedback with a plain reduced-motion swap.
Natural-height content lane with exact 14/600 title, 13/400 supporting copy, 3 px gap, and 2/3-line defaults.
Baseline-aligned helper, validation, and optional mono counter row with canonical warning anatomy.
Compatibility content stack for explicit-save text editors, ordered as supporting metadata, feedback, and secondary action before the commit bar.
Full-width, non-wrapping trailing Cancel/Done group with optional leading content. While visible during an explicit save, Done owns the field's sole progress indicator.
Full-row clipped height-and-opacity disclosure sibling that isolates nested pointer and focus interactions from the header.
Fixed 800 ms Phosphor spinner shared by mutually exclusive 16 px trailing-status and 13 px visible commit-saving placements.
Layout-neutral immediate 2 px outer focus ring with a 2 px transparent gap for field-family atoms.
Field-owned wrapping single- or multi-choice control with staged selection and canonical commit actions.
Field-owned vertical single-select control that composes one complete CatchOptionCard title-and-description target per explanatory option.

Exact 14 px / 600 single-line field-choice tile whose visual and hit box are identical, with primaryInk selected content, selected check, pressed scale, 8 px wrapped-run spacing, Enter/Space activation, and a focus-visible ring.

Bounded minus-value-plus control with 15 px glyphs, complete 44 px hit areas around 32 px visuals, immediate pointer-down stepping, touch drag-exit cancellation, an immediate outer keyboard-focus ring, and acceleration after the tenth repeat tick.

Field-owned 14 px / 600 Cancel or Done pill with exact 18 by 10 padding, primaryInk Done content, animated saving opacity, a 13 px spinner separated from Saving ellipsis by 7 px, sole progress ownership while its explicit commit bar is visible, and an immediate 2 px outer keyboard-focus ring at a 2 px offset.
Exact 44 by 26 field switch with animated track, knob, and saving opacity plus Enter/Space activation and a focus-visible ring.
Stepper-owned 44 px repeat target with a 32 px visual circle, immediate pointer-down step, pointer-up and touch drag-exit cancellation, 400 ms delay, 110 ms repeats, and 55 ms repeats after tick 10.
Direct optional-badge renderer used inside form field labels.

notes: CatchField is the only global field primitive. Its row anatomy is leadingIcon? | label/value column | trailingSlot?, and title/body emphasis is controlled by CatchFieldEmphasis.

Use the named constructors read, content, nav, action, toggle, input, inputActions, control, choices, optionCards, stepper, select, and add so new call sites select one field capability explicitly. CatchField.content maps React body semantics without changing legacy Flutter body-as-value rows. Existing actions and deprecated expanding callers are retained as source-compatible inputs to the same renderer.

Trailing affordances are derived by the constructor contract: read, action, and direct input never synthesize a chevron; actionable nav derives a fixed chevron; control, choices, stepper, inputActions, and select derive rotating disclosure glyphs. Existing nav callers may still supply showChevron while they migrate to structural action or nav constructors.

Explicit-save compatibility order is editor, supporting metadata, feedback, secondary action, then Cancel and Done. Validation/helper/counter support is a root sibling after disclosure controls and the commit bar, matching the React handoff. Field-caption color is resolved once at the root: actual error uses danger, active focus or open disclosure uses semantic ink, and inactive state uses the configured tone or ink3. Optional suffix copy remains ink3. Focus never changes label size, weight, baseline, or position.

Press feedback begins on header pointer down; focus or disclosure ownership transfers once on pointer up. The full-row disclosure drawer is a sibling below that header, so nested chips, steppers, and commit buttons cannot trigger header press tint and retain complete hit/focus geometry. The pressed and active states animate one opaque surface rather than flickering between layers. CatchFieldInsetScope separates content-gutter ownership from active-overlay bleed: divided rows retain their wider lift overlap, while contained edge rows overlap the section's consumed border inset by exactly one hairline so both primitives paint one shared left/right edge. Explicit-save tap-away cancels on pointer up only when movement remains within touch slop, while drag/scroll leaves the field open and loading blocks Cancel or Escape. Ordinary inputs retain Flutter's shared EditableText tap group for affiliated actions and place the native cursor during the first gesture. Disclosure content stays mounted behind a clipped height-factor plus opacity reveal, uses CatchFieldTokens.reveal, and becomes instantaneous when reduced motion is enabled.

CatchField.content always owns title/supporting-copy typography. Its default body emphasis preserves the React handoff's top-aligned leading/trailing slots; callers may opt into title emphasis when a combined content block and trailing action should center together.

Choice chips use a compact full-width wrapping layout, identical visual/hit boxes, and an 8 px visual run gap; explicit-save choices preserve staged selection until Done, while a single choice without a save bar closes after 180 ms. Steppers keep their value centered between bounded 44 px repeat targets; toggle tracks are 44 by 26 px, stay visible but disabled while saving, and vertically center with the leading icon and text lane.

CatchField.choices owns collapsed choice summaries when body is omitted: selected values are filtered in source option order and joined with ' · '. An explicit body remains the compatibility override for custom formatting.

CatchField.optionCards is the single-select explanatory counterpart to choices: the collapsed row derives the selected title, while every expanded option keeps its title and description inside one full-width CatchOptionCard target. Use chips only when labels are terse and do not need per-option explanation.
isOptional is visible and semantic copy only. allowEmptySelection independently governs whether the final selected option may be removed for single- and multi-choice controls.

Every empty inactive editable row uses one canonical value lane: primary 14/600 'Add {field}' plus an optional inline ink3/500 suffix, with no separate caption. Direct and explicit-save text paths keep the same native editable mounted behind that presentation; focus restores the caption, input-only hint, and input-only units without a second tap or cursor reset. Addable disclosures use the same typography, row height, and icon alignment.

A populated labeled direct input reserves and overlays its 24 px clear hit target; the target must never participate in intrinsic collapsed-row height. Label-less compact clear inputs retain their own control geometry.

Disclosure opening drives the nearest existing vertical scroll owner frame by frame with the same CatchFieldTokens.reveal duration and curve, revealing the commit bar above CatchFieldVisibilityScope bottom obstruction as new extent becomes available, with a disclosure-bottom fallback when no commit bar exists. Rapid close cancels the drive, direct user scrolling wins, reduced motion jumps immediately, and a zero-duration final correction covers any residual collapsed-extent clamp. CatchField never creates or owns a ScrollController.

Implemented handoff tranche: dedicated 14/600 title plus 13/400 supporting-copy content rows with 2/3 clamps and a 3 px gap while legacy value rows retain 1/2 clamps; 14/500/1.5 multiline row input; 16 px caret; 600-weight chip/Add labels; 15 px stepper glyph; 16/13 px spinners with a 7 px commit gap and 800 ms cadence; filled saved and warning error icons; directional underline sweep; 200 ms control-opacity reveal; animated toggle/Cancel states; post-tick-10 repeat acceleration; hardware-keyboard activation; immediate transparent-offset focus-visible rings; primaryInk selected/action foregrounds; and exact support/value typography.

Owner-gated behavior remains unchanged for single-choice persistence, clearability, deselection, and save policy. The implemented 180 ms no-save-bar close behavior and standardized empty Add presentation are not authorization to change domain persistence semantics.
CatchField does not own rounded section or input-container chrome; use CatchSection contained focus/error chrome when fields need a grouped rounded surface.
Field labels and support/error text belong to catch.field; shared container chrome belongs to CatchSection.

components.name: CatchSection
components.summary: Canonical information-grouping primitive for divided field rows, exact contained field-row surfaces with shared child-edge geometry, generic contained sections, and plain titled content groups. Call sites use named constructors so header, divider, focus, and surface ownership stay explicit.

slots.name: children
slots.notes: Use CatchField children for information rows; use contained when a rounded group is needed and divided when the page should stay flatter.

slots.name: child
slots.notes: Use child for custom section bodies that are already internally composed.

members.summary: Standard section title row with optional trailing action, uppercase, heavy, and custom padding/style states.
Activity-accent eyebrow label for section introductions with optional leading glyph and bounded text.

Public contained-section focus and error chrome member. Generic contained sections may reflect descendant focus; field-row surfaces publish the one-hairline active-overlay overlap themselves, keep descendant focus child-owned, align active child chrome to the same perimeter geometry, paint the section edge last, and change perimeter color only for explicit focused or error state.
Chronological section header for day-grouped feeds with optional count and sticky sliver delegate support.
Direct animated numeric count renderer used by CatchDaySectionHeader.
Titled horizontal item rail with optional trailing item and section divider.
Titled vertical list section with standard section header and separated embedded rows.

notes: Use divided sections for top-level information groups when nested rounded rectangles would add visual clutter.

Use containedFieldRows for the handoff field surface: it owns a clipped 14 px radius, neutral line border, explicit section-focus/error border, inset internal rules, and no generic elevation. Descendant field focus remains child-owned; the section publishes a one-hairline active-overlay overlap so child chrome and the outer perimeter share identical left/right geometry, then paints the section edge last. Its optional title, right-aligned count, and trailing slot use 14 px / 16 px / 2 px in-card header padding; its optional footer stays inside the border with 2 px / 16 px / 12 px padding. Divided fieldRows footers render outside the rows with 8 px top padding and the field-caption style. Headerless prompt cards begin directly with their first field row. Use contained for non-field carded content that needs one bounded surface.
Use plain sections for compact titled blocks without a container or divider.

The section owns the outer grouping and internal dividers. Outer section rules use the full line token; boundaries between sibling field rows use the muted fieldRow tone and remain coverable by an active field's 10 px bleed. Direct CatchField children derive a zero or text-lane divider inset from their leading icons; adapter-wrapped rows pass dividerInset explicitly. Fields own information content and interaction mode.

Divided and contained field-row headers accept title, count, and trailing content. Contained footers use 2 px top padding; divided footers use 8 px. Field-section kickers use the exact 11 px, 600-weight, 1.43 px tracking treatment.
Section headers, section labels, day headers, vertical sections, and horizontal rails are public members of this grouping family, not separate section systems.

components.name: CatchSkeleton
components.summary: Content-shaped placeholder concept for known loading layouts, including list and screen/sliver placement adapters.

slots.name: child
slots.notes: Use only when the named shapes cannot match the eventual content shape.

members.summary: Vertical list of content-shaped skeleton cards with standard spacing.
Screen-body placement adapter that composes CatchSkeletonList with CatchScreenBody.
Sliver placement adapter that composes CatchSkeletonList with CatchSliverPageBody.

notes: Prefer content-shaped skeletons when the eventual layout is known.
Async screen and sliver loading are placement adapters over CatchSkeletonList, not separate visual concepts.

components.name: CatchLoadingIndicator
components.summary: Indeterminate progress indicator for unknown, compact, or one-off work where content-shaped placeholders are not useful.

notes: Use for tiny inline operations, platform or plugin waits, and unknown one-off work.
Use catch.skeleton when the eventual content layout is known.

components.name: CatchAsyncValueView
components.summary: AsyncValue data, loading, and error boundary with branded defaults for box and sliver layouts.

members.summary: Sliver-native AsyncValue boundary for CustomScrollView bodies with branded loading and error defaults.

notes: This concept owns async data-state switching, not a loading visual system.
Use the sliver member only inside sliver-native scroll layouts.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchStartupLoadingScreen
components.summary: Branded app-start composition with the Catch logo and a delayed bounded loading indicator.

notes: This is a reusable branded startup composition, not a fourth loading concept.
The delayed indicator prevents brief startup work from flashing progress chrome.

components.name: CatchKicker
components.summary: Public text-role atom family for uppercase kicker labels and single-line mono metadata labels.

members.summary: Single-line mono metadata label for cards, rails, distances, and compact facts.

notes: Use these widgets for repeated text-role atoms; keep raw text-style definitions centralized in CatchTextStyles.
Do not create feature-local typography wrappers for kicker or mono metadata labels.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchMiniBarChart
components.summary: Compact mini bar chart for dense metric panels and data-coverage summaries.

notes: Use only for compact trend or coverage summaries; larger analytical charts need a dedicated data visualization surface.
Always provide a semantic label when the chart communicates non-decorative data.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchMetricStrip
components.summary: Canonical compact value-over-label metric rail for detail stats and summary data pairs.

members.summary: Vertical value-over-label metric member for compact stats, highlighted values, optional icons, centered alignment, and surfaced single-stat blocks.
Dot-separated inline metadata row for event and club cards with optional icons and trailing strong metadata.
Direct dot-separated metadata flow renderer used by CatchMetaDotRow.
Direct metadata entry renderer for optional icon, color override, strong trailing weight, and ellipsis behavior.
Direct metric-strip value/label cell renderer.
Direct metric-strip divider renderer.

notes: Use for compact quantitative or short factual data pairs on detail surfaces.
Keep labels short and sentence/lowercase unless product copy explicitly requires an acronym.
Use CatchMetricStripItem.unit for a short unit such as km instead of embedding it in the value.
Use CatchStatColumn for single or wrapped vertical stats; keep it in the metric family rather than creating bespoke value-label columns.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchBottomSheetScaffold
components.summary: Canonical sheet-shell family for rounded bottom-sheet surfaces, grabber handles, draggable persistent shells, and share-card sheet presentation.

members.summary: Centered sheet drag affordance used by bottom-sheet and draggable-sheet shells.
Persistent draggable sheet shell with optional handle, top radius, and raised shadow.
Share-card sheet that captures a slotted card preview and delegates image sharing to an injected ExternalShareController.
Direct plain sheet-header renderer for title, subtitle, badge, and trailing content.
Direct branded sheet-header renderer with glyph, title, subtitle, badge, and trailing content.

notes: Use for modal and persistent bottom-sheet surfaces before creating feature-local sheet shells.
Keep bottom CTA/footer bars separate from sheet shells unless the API is explicitly merged.

components.name: CatchIconButton
components.summary: Circular icon action for toolbar, overlay, and plain glyph-button affordances.

slots.name: child
slots.notes: Prefer Icon(CatchIcons.*); the primitive owns glyph size and color.

notes: Design handoff must include tooltip/action label when used as an accessible command.
Use CatchIconButton.counted for integer overlays; zero hides and values above 99 clamp through CatchCountBadge.

components.name: CatchMenu
components.summary: Canonical anchored menu family for overflow actions, row selection, disabled rows, destructive actions, and typed callbacks.

members.summary: Direct menu-row renderer for selected, disabled, danger, icon, sublabel, and checkmark states.
Anchored overflow trigger that opens CatchMenu from a CatchIconButton.

notes: Use CatchActionMenu for icon-triggered overflow menus and CatchMenu for direct menu panel review.
Keep selected, disabled, and destructive row states in this family instead of creating feature-local menu rows.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchIconTile
components.summary: Decorative square icon tile for quick actions, data cards, and compact labeled rows where the icon is content rather than a tappable command.

notes: Use for non-interactive icon-leading content blocks.
Use CatchIconButton when the glyph itself is a command.

components.name: CatchControlShell
components.summary: Shared one-line control chrome for fields, selects, steppers, search controls, and compact picker triggers.

notes: Use as internal chrome for control primitives; product UI should usually enter through the higher-level field, search, select, or stepper APIs.
Do not fork control borders, focus rings, or compact heights in feature widgets.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchNumberStepper
components.summary: Canonical numeric increment/decrement control with bounded step logic, accessible tooltips, and Catch control-shell chrome.

notes: Use for bounded numeric choices where plus/minus adjustment is faster than text entry.
Callers own domain limits and mutation timing; the primitive only disables impossible local steps.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchOptionCard
components.summary: Selectable explanatory choice card for mutually exclusive options with a sentence of supporting copy.

notes: Use Chip or SelectChip for terse filter labels; use OptionCard when the option needs explanation.

components.name: CatchSurface
components.summary: Canonical low-level surface primitive for base surfaces, bounded cards, tinted notes, inline messages, and tappable tiles.

members.summary: Shared internal/public row grammar behind message surfaces and inline feedback banners.

notes: CatchSurface is the only low-level surface primitive. Use CatchSurface.card, CatchSurface.tinted, and CatchSurface.message for surface chrome; use CatchSection.contained for section-card content chrome.
Do not hand-roll Container/DecoratedBox cards in feature UI; request a Surface mode if this contract is missing a state.
InlineMessageSurface is a member of catch.surface message grammar and should not become a separate surface family.

replacementMap.SliverAppBar: a registered media-hero sliver or CatchTopBar

components.name: CatchTopBar
components.summary: Canonical app-bar primitive for compact/large title chrome, back/close leading, trailing actions, and expanding search.

members.summary: Root-screen title stack for Home, Explore, Chats, Profile, Activity, and Settings using Archivo headline typography with optional eyebrow, subtitle, leading, action, material, and padding slots.
Root-screen app-bar wrapper that composes CatchScreenHeaderTitle inside CatchTopBar while preserving actions, leading, safe area, padding, bottom, and expanding search configuration.
Toolbar title that fades in only after a flexible sliver header collapses.
Top-bar-scoped icon action wrapper that composes CatchIconButton with navigation sizing and tooltip semantics.
Top-bar-scoped overflow menu action that delegates to CatchActionMenu.
PreferredSize tab segment used as CatchTopBar bottom chrome.
Top-bar-scoped text action wrapper that composes CatchTextButton with route-chrome color defaults.

notes: Use this primitive for route chrome before creating local AppBar variants.
Use CatchScreenHeaderTitle or CatchScreenTopBar for root screen headers that need the main tab Archivo title voice.
Large mode is implied by a non-empty kicker unless explicitly overridden.
Use CatchTopBar.identity for conversation or profile-backed app bars; missing identity taps are runtime availability, not a separate design variant.
Top-bar root header helpers, action wrappers, and collapsed sliver titles are typed members of catch.top_bar; use them inside route chrome rather than creating local app-bar helper widgets.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchPrivacyBadge
components.summary: Localized privacy/visibility adapter that maps domain meaning into CatchBadge.privacy.

notes: Use only for privacy/visibility semantics; generic status belongs in CatchBadge.
The enum adapter owns localized copy and icon mapping while CatchBadge.privacy owns all chrome.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchJourneySteps
components.summary: Numbered, line-traced sequence for ordered onboarding, dashboard, or event journey steps.

members.summary: Direct journey-step node renderer for step status, icon, and connector composition.

notes: Use only when order and trace matter; unordered benefits or facts should use a list/card pattern.
The primitive owns numbering, rail geometry, and connector termination.

components.name: CatchScreenBody
components.summary: Standard non-sliver screen body wrapper that owns page gutters, vertical padding, and optional scrolling.

members.summary: Form-step body wrapper with the same page rhythm and gutter ownership as CatchScreenBody.
Padding-only page body member for screens that already own scrolling.
Sliver placement member for page-body padding inside CustomScrollView screens.

notes: Use for regular non-sliver route bodies before composing ad hoc page padding.
Screens may override top/bottom rhythm but should not hand-roll the horizontal gutter.
Screen-body governance covers non-sliver and sliver placement members; screens should not hand-roll page gutters or form-step padding.

components.name: CatchSectionStack
components.summary: Standard stack wrapper for handoff sections, owning page padding and optional inserted gaps between section blocks.

slots.name: children
slots.notes: Prefer CatchSection children for handoff sections; simple primitives are allowed for compact internal stacks.

members.summary: Sliver-native detail-page section list with standard detail gutter and section-owned rhythm.
Column helper for ordered section blocks with configurable inserted rhythm.

notes: Use under CatchScreenBody or sliver wrappers to prevent screen-local section spacing drift.
Let CatchSection own section dividers; use the gap prop only for explicit non-handoff stacks.
SectionList and DetailSliverSectionList are public members of catch.section_stack for placement-specific layout, not separate section systems.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchRosterTiles
components.summary: Selectable host-roster count tiles for filtering participant boards by lifecycle or attention state.

notes: Use for roster filtering or count summaries only; do not use as a generic stat strip.
Parent state owns selection and filtering; the visual primitive only emits selected ids.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchRosterRow
components.summary: Host-roster participant row with identity, signal badge, and typed action cell.

slots.name: action
slots.notes: Use CatchRosterButtonAction, CatchRosterDecideAction, CatchRosterBadgeAction, or CatchRosterTextAction.

notes: Rows should receive immutable display data and typed callbacks from a roster adapter.
Use action-cell variants instead of ad hoc trailing widgets so table alignment remains stable.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchRosterTable
components.summary: Host-roster table shell with fixed identity/signal/action columns and built-in empty state.

slots.name: rows
slots.notes: Rows should be CatchRosterRow instances so the fixed 5/3/3 column contract holds.

notes: Use for host roster board tables; compact metrics and filters should stay in CatchRosterTiles.
The table owns headers and empty state, while callers own filtering, mutation, and data loading.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchActivityArt
components.summary: Activity-pigmented art shell with gradient, motif texture, ghost glyph, optional dim layer, and overlay child.

slots.name: child
slots.notes: Use for foreground story content; the art shell still owns pigment and clipping.

notes: Use as the canonical activity-gradient art shell.
Do not create feature-local gradient/photo fallback shells without first checking whether this can compose the surface.

components.name: CatchNetworkImage
components.summary: Canonical remote and bundled image primitive with decode sizing, branded fallback, loading hook, and semantic-label support.

members.summary: Direct branded image fallback renderer used by network and asset image loading failures.

notes: All remote images should route through this primitive for cache sizing and branded fallback behavior.
Clipping, aspect ratio, and circular/rounded framing remain caller-owned layout decisions.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchDistanceRing
components.summary: Map-adjacent radius ring with optional mono label and tappable label semantics.

members.summary: Tappable mono edge label shared by static and native geographic distance rings.

notes: Use for Flutter-rendered radius visuals; native map overlays remain platform-specific but should match this visual grammar.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchCodeInput
components.summary: Static one-time-code visual row for handoff previews and externally managed code-entry state.

members.summary: Real one-time-code text input that composes CatchCodeInput visual cells over one hidden platform TextField.
Direct code-input row renderer for fixed-length verification cells.
Direct single code-input cell renderer.
Direct active-caret renderer used inside code-input cells.

notes: Use CatchOtpCodeField for real platform text input; it composes this visual primitive over one hidden TextField.
CatchOtpCodeField is the platform input member of catch.code_input; CatchCodeInput remains the pure visual row.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchOptionGroup
components.summary: Underline option group for inline scope controls, lens tabs, and compact mode pickers.

slots.name: trailing
slots.notes: Use only for compact metadata or a single adjacent action.

members.summary: Direct underline option renderer used by CatchOptionGroup.

notes: Use as the canonical single-select scope and mode row across Flutter surfaces, including lifecycle, audience, granularity, and workspace section switching.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchTabRail
components.summary: Preferred-size app-bar bottom rail that wraps CatchOptionGroup in the standard tab-rail shell.

members.summary: Binds a route-owned TabController to the canonical CatchTabRail, including pager interpolation.

notes: Use for app-bar bottom tabs. Use CatchOptionGroup directly for inline scope controls that are not PreferredSizeWidget app-bar bottoms.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchSearchField
components.summary: Canonical search input, including pill field mode and expanding/collapsed search affordance mode.

notes: Do not register a separate ExpandingSearch contract; expanding and expanded modes live here.

components.name: CatchRangeSlider
components.summary: Canonical dual-thumb range slider with Catch track, thumb, endpoint label, and semantic-value styling.

notes: Feature screens should not theme raw RangeSlider directly.

components.name: CatchToggle
components.summary: Canonical settings toggle control with animated pill track, primary on fill, quiet off track, surface knob, disabled opacity, Enter/Space activation, and a 2 px focus-visible ring.

notes: Use CatchField toggle mode for settings rows; use CatchToggle for the control itself. The control animates track, knob, and disabled opacity with reduced-motion support, activates from Enter or Space, and exposes a 2 px focus-visible ring without adding a second field-row focus target.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchStatusBar
components.summary: Phone-frame status row used for handoff previews and screenshot framing, not product navigation chrome.

notes: Use only in device preview frames; production app chrome comes from the OS and app shell.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchStepHeader
components.summary: Wizard/step-flow header built from CatchTopBar large mode plus an optional progress hairline.

slots.name: trailing
slots.notes: Use for one compact metadata/action element only.

members.summary: Hairline step-progress indicator used by wizard and step-flow headers.

notes: Use for multi-step flows; do not create thin feature wrappers unless they add real behavior.
CatchStepProgress is the progress member of catch.step_header, not a separate wizard primitive.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchTabBar
components.summary: Adaptive bottom navigation bar with icon-only unselected tabs, selected icon-label pill reveal, badges, and platform-specific chrome.

members.summary: Direct tab-bar button renderer with selected-label reveal semantics.
Direct tab-bar icon/count renderer.

notes: Use for Catch bottom navigation. Platform adaptation belongs inside this widget; do not fork native/custom tab bars in shells.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchBottomDock
components.summary: Anchored bottom utility surface for chat inputs, filter actions, and compact controls above the device safe area.

notes: Use CatchBottomDock only for generic anchored utility chrome. Primary screen CTAs route through CatchBottomAction.
Use CatchTabBar for bottom navigation; do not merge tab navigation behavior into this utility dock.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchConfirmDialog
components.summary: Material Catch confirmation dialog body used by the adaptive confirm-dialog launcher on non-Cupertino platforms.

notes: Use showCatchAdaptiveDialog for runtime presentation so iOS can use CupertinoAlertDialog.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchFormDialog
components.summary: Catch dialog shell for compact form bodies with title, slotted form content, and optional trailing action row.

notes: Use for compact modal forms whose body is already composed from field/section primitives.
Use CatchConfirmDialog for typed confirmation choices; do not overload FormDialog for simple message confirmations.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchCountPill
components.summary: Labelled floating action with a required callback, optional icon, and typed count overlay.

notes: Use for labelled floating actions only; use CatchIconButton for icon-only commands.
Counts are integers rendered by CatchCountBadge; raw string badges are not accepted.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchPageDots
components.summary: Compact page/progress dot indicator with selected-width emphasis, semantic label support, and motion-token transitions.

notes: Use for carousel/page progress only; use CatchStepProgress for wizard completion progress.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: ClubDetailDock
components.summary: Club-detail membership dock with role-specific guest, visitor, member, and owner action states.

notes: This is a product composition, not a generic bottom bar.
Retire feature-local membership buttons when this dock can own the role/action state.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchCoverStory
components.summary: Explore cover/masthead composition with dark editorial ground, activity glow, optional chrome, CTA, and data lines.

notes: This is an Explore editorial composition. Resolve live Explore header duplication before broadening the contract.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchPersonAvatar
components.summary: Canonical person/avatar bubble for photos, deterministic initials, activity-context fallback, rings, status dots, obscured states, square host shapes, and overflow counts.

props.name: count
props.notes: Only available through CatchPersonAvatar.count.

members.summary: Stacked person-avatar composition with overlap, border, and overflow-count handling.
Direct person-avatar shell renderer for shape, image, fallback, and adornment composition.
Direct obscured-avatar content renderer.
Direct veiled-avatar renderer for obscured people in stacks and cards.
Direct activity-context initials placeholder renderer.
Direct initials placeholder renderer for person avatars.

notes: Do not reintroduce ActivityAvatar as a separate contract; activity-context fallback is part of CatchPersonAvatar.
Avatar stacks are composition members of catch.person_avatar rather than a separate avatar family.

components.name: CatchRowPressSurface
components.summary: Full-width hover, focus, pressed, tap, and semantic interaction layer for list and field rows.

notes: The child owns content layout and padding; this primitive owns only the full-row interaction layer.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchPersonRow
components.summary: Global people row for roster, safety, and chat-preview surfaces with configurable avatar shape, divider, timestamp, unread badge, and new-match marker.

members.summary: Direct chat-row layout renderer used by CatchPersonRow chat variants.
Direct chat-row trailing metadata renderer.
Direct unread-count pill renderer used by chat rows.
Direct new-match dot renderer used by chat rows.
Direct roster-row layout renderer used by CatchPersonRow roster variants.

notes: Global product composition for people rows. Feature view models should map into CatchPersonRowData at the screen/list boundary rather than introducing row adapters.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchEventCard
components.summary: Canonical ticket-style event card surface backed by shared activity visuals and event media atoms.

members.summary: Event media member that renders uploaded event imagery with the global photo grade or falls back to activity artwork and configurable scrims.
Shared activity artwork backdrop used by event cards, thumbnails, and no-photo event media states.
Circular activity glyph stamp used by event cards, agenda rows, and ticket compositions.
Ticket-card divider member for the event ticket metaphor.
Event-card time glyph member used by ticket and agenda compositions.
Event-card status pill member for booked, nearby, waitlist, and other event state labels.
Compact list and map DateTicket with an activity rail, organizer and event identity hierarchy, one optional context block, and a trailing price decision row.
Shared responsive ticket decision row for time, capacity, status, and trailing price copy.
Direct event-thumbnail activity fallback renderer for no-photo states.
Direct event-thumbnail scrim overlay renderer.

notes: Ticket, spotlight, and compact are named constructors on one event card API, not separate public card classes.
CatchEventThumbnail is an event-card media member rather than a competing product concept.
Use activity visuals for no-photo event states so cards, thumbnails, map sheets, and detail headers share event-type color semantics.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchGradedImage
components.summary: Global display-time image grade that gives uploaded and generated imagery the shared Catch visual treatment without mutating source media.

notes: Use this for the global Catch image treatment across event, profile, club, and upload surfaces.
Do not fork event-specific image grading; event media should compose this primitive through CatchEventThumbnail.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchDetailHeroBackdrop
components.summary: Canonical detail-page media backdrop state for photo-backed and branded-fallback heroes with optional scrim treatment.

members.summary: Direct detail-hero fallback renderer for no-photo media states.
Shared photo scrim renderer for detail heroes, photo frames, and profile hero tinting.

notes: Treat this as the media state of detail heroes, not as a separate event-card concept.
Use when a detail header needs a shared photo-or-branded-fallback backdrop before screen-specific title/chrome is composed.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchActivityMapPin
components.summary: Canonical activity-colored map pin for resting and selected map marks with optional uppercased selected-state flag text.

notes: Use for activity map marks; do not create feature-specific pin glyphs for Explore or event maps unless the map interaction introduces a genuinely new state.
Selected flag labels are uppercased at the primitive boundary and native map surfaces should render byte-backed markers from the same token metrics.

components.name: EventDetailSections
components.summary: Event Detail screen-section contract that groups the event-specific hero, ticket facts, overview primitives, host/social sections, companion prompt, and booking dock under one Catch-owned handoff boundary.

props.name: callbacks
props.notes: Route-owned save/share/calendar/location/club/message/companion/retry callbacks.

members.summary: Event detail hero section with standard, ticket, and spotlight-dark media treatments plus route-owned top actions.
Flush ticket fact band for when, where, pace, and activity facts below the hero.
Why-you-might-click hint list derived from event format and activity context.
Timed event itinerary rail for route plan, meet, run, and post-event moments.
Canonical event-detail photo proof strip with uploaded photos and activity-soft placeholders.
Event detail map preview card with exact or morning-of pin states and optional route-owned tap action.
How-sign-ups-work list for open signup, approval, waitlist, and demand-pricing mechanics.
Good-to-know list for requirements, expectations, booking, cancellation, and settlement rows.
Provider-free host section adapter for hidden, loading, error, and host-card content states.
Who's-going and reviews section composition for guest lock, member roster, review visibility, and attended review action states.
Provider-free companion prompt adapter for hidden, loading, error, and available Event Success plan states.
Provider-free booking dock renderer for eligible, paid, booked, waitlist, waitlist-offer, attended, past, disabled, pending, and error states.
Event-detail attendee-hype avatar stack that composes the shared Catch person-avatar stack for hidden and revealed roster states.

notes: Use this screen contract for Event Detail section handoffs. Reusable primitives stay in their existing catch.* contracts; event-specific compounds remain members here until they prove reusable outside Event Detail.
Route-owned provider reads, booking mutations, save/share/calendar side effects, and navigation callbacks stay outside visual members.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: NotificationRow
components.summary: Activity notification list row with type glyph, read/unread emphasis, timestamp, optional body, divider, and tap semantics.

notes: Product composition for notification rows; keep row semantics tied to notification type/read state.

components.name: ChatInputBar
components.summary: Canonical floating chat composer with a multiline field and matching 44px image/send actions; derives focus and sendability locally and keeps unrelated controls usable during pending operations.

notes: Both actions use CatchIconButton.defaultSize; composer call sites cannot supply independent image/send geometry.
Empty text disables Send automatically; callbacks describe capability, not draft validity.
Text-send and image-upload pending states disable only their relevant action and do not disable or defocus the editor.
Upload failures remain route-owned transient feedback through CatchMutationErrorListeners; do not add feature-local inline error chrome.

components.name: CatchTabbedScreenScaffold
components.summary: Canonical root-screen shell for a scroll-away identity title, pinned option rail, native horizontal pager, and independently restored vertical tab pages.

slots.name: body
slots.notes: The route supplies a TabBarView bound to the same controller as the rail.

members.summary: Per-page vertical owner for focus isolation, matching overlap injection, stable PageStorage restoration, and shell-aware terminal padding.
Sliver-native semantic width policy that stays direct on phones and centers the canonical readable lane on wide viewports.

notes: The route owns the typed TabController and TabBarView; the shell owns title, pinned rail, overlap, and safe-area composition.
Every page uses CatchTabbedPageScrollView with a stable PageStorageKey. Never switch tab children inside one shared vertical ListView.
Feature code retains async state, data policy, route actions, and specialized inner-scroll bridges.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchBottomAction
components.summary: Canonical platform-adaptive primary bottom action: inset floating chrome on Cupertino platforms and an anchored full-width action bar on Material platforms.

members.summary: Provider-free action contents shared by the floating and anchored platform shells.

notes: Callers provide one CTA contract; CatchBottomAction selects floating Cupertino or anchored Material chrome from the active platform.
Do not place primary screen CTAs in CatchBottomDock or branch on platform at feature call sites.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchHostRow
components.summary: Compact provider-free host or organizer identity row whose message and navigation affordances are derived directly from the available callbacks.

notes: Supplying onTap automatically renders the navigation chevron; omitting onTap makes navigation chrome impossible.
Supplying onMessage automatically renders the message action and requires messageTooltip, so affordance and capability cannot drift.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchMapPreview
components.summary: Read-only, attribution-safe Google Maps viewport for compact location previews with one exact marker and a deterministic no-network fallback.

notes: Use CatchMapPreview inside compact cards; it disables gestures and controls, preserves Google attribution space, and enables Android lite mode automatically.
Use enableNetworkTiles=false only for deterministic tests, Widgetbook, captures, or offline review; production call sites default to real Google tiles.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchMetaRow
components.summary: Single icon-and-label metadata line for compact factual context; distinct from structural section labels.

notes: Use for a factual icon-and-label line; use catch.ui_label for structural section hierarchy.
The CatchMetaRow/StageSectionLabel ledger decision is an intentional false-positive boundary.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchIndexRow
components.summary: Canonical hairline index row for compact directories and browse lists with optional leading and trailing content.

notes: Use for quiet browse and directory indexes; richer selectable controls belong to catch.option_group.

waiver.reason: No distinct steering or placement rule is required for this canonical component in enforcement v1.

components.name: CatchClubCover
components.summary: Canonical club-cover resolver for uploaded photos, the global image grade, deterministic club artwork, and compact fallbacks.

notes: Use whenever a club cover is rendered; do not resolve imageUrl, clubPhotos, or fallback artwork independently at call sites.
